decisions.title: Ship Canvas V2 prototype for internal dogfooding
decisions.context: Canvas is a key 2026 product pillar under 'Build the Moat'. DES-139 and DES-141 show active UX work. Need to decide go/no-go for internal release by end of Q1 to maintain momentum and gather feedback before external beta.

arguments.for: Early internal feedback will accelerate iteration cycles and validate Signal>Evidence>Decision workflow for non-rules use cases. Aligns with Embed Deeper pillar by showing new interaction models.
arguments.against: UX work still in prototype phase (DES-139, DES-141). Rushing could create poor first impressions with internal users. May need more design iteration before dogfooding.

decisions.title: Prioritize FedRAMP compliance work vs. core feature development
decisions.context: Multiple FedRAMP tickets active (ENG-9684, ENG-9688, ENG-9695, ENG-9694) indicate compliance is consuming engineering capacity. Need to balance compliance requirements with Nail the Core pillar and North Star metric growth.
decisions.blockedBy: Unclear scope of remaining FedRAMP requirements and customer contract commitments. Need compliance roadmap and resource model.

arguments.for: FedRAMP unlocks government customers (Embed Deeper). Compliance work is table stakes for enterprise expansion. Delaying risks contract penalties.
arguments.against: Core product velocity is suffering. 10+ tickets on infrastructure vs. user-facing features. Not directly tied to North Star metric. Could defer non-critical compliance items.

decisions.title: Commit to Explore V2 GA date based on current table/panel polish work
decisions.context: Significant polish work in flight: ENG-9277 (table improvements pre-GA), ENG-9593 (LegendV2), ENG-9061 (metrics panel), ENG-9643 (table bugs). Explore V2 is foundational to Nail the Core. Need GA commitment to align org.

arguments.for: Polish work suggests near-GA quality. Setting date creates urgency and focus. GA milestone boosts customer confidence and sales velocity. Directly enables more Decisions Captured.
arguments.against: Still seeing foundational bugs (ENG-9681, ENG-9643) and UX improvements (DES-155 hovercard). Premature GA could damage reputation. Need clearer definition of 'done'.

decisions.title: Sunset legacy PTSID service as part of storage refactor
decisions.context: ENG-9651 explicitly removes PTSID service. Major architectural change. Storage refactor tickets (ENG-9659, ENG-9655, ENG-9268, ENG-9666, ENG-9649) show significant backend evolution. Need migration plan and customer impact assessment.

arguments.for: Legacy service adds complexity and maintenance burden. New L0/L1 architecture (ENG-9268, ENG-9666) is more scalable and performant. Supports Build the Moat through better infrastructure. Enables per-org isolation (ENG-9655).
arguments.against: High-risk change affecting core data path. No clear rollback plan visible. Could impact uptime SLAs. May require customer coordination for migration windows.

decisions.title: Invest in Python Rules productization vs. new feature exploration
decisions.context: Python Rules is listed as key 2026 product but no related tickets visible in this signal batch. Heavy focus on infrastructure (storage, FedRAMP) and Explore polish. Need to assess if Python Rules is getting adequate investment.

arguments.for: Python Rules directly enables Decisions Captured (North Star). Listed as strategic product. If under-invested, risks missing 2026 targets. Should redirect resources from lower-priority work.
arguments.against: Possible that Python Rules work is in design phase or under different ticket naming. Infrastructure investments (storage, FedRAMP) may be prerequisites. Need signal review before reallocating resources.

timeAudit.weekSummary: Heavy meeting load (80% scheduled). Multiple cross-functional syncs on FedRAMP, Canvas, and storage refactor. Low focus time impacting decision velocity.
alerts: 40+ engineering tickets in flight suggests over-allocation or unclear prioritization
No Python Rules signals visible despite being listed as strategic 2026 product
FedRAMP compliance consuming ~15% of eng capacity across 4+ tickets
Storage refactor spans 6+ tickets across multiple squads - needs integration plan
Canvas UX work (DES-139, 141, 155) not clearly tied to eng implementation tickets
